ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: Ɛmɔ hɔ́ ndɛ-ndɛ bɛ hán héle yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ ɔ fi ewue nun ɔ'a tinnge. Sian dɩɛ, ɔ lɛ kɔ a nwʋnndɛ ɛmɔ Galile mân nɩn anun. Ɛlɔ yɛ̂ ɛmɔ 'nwun yɩ́ ɔ. Ngalɩɛ'n mɔ mɩ́n nwan mɩn 'kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ nɩn anɩn.”
translated: It was while they were still there that Jesus told them, “Go and tell John what you have seen and heard: the blind see, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the poor are given the Good News.”

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: Nan wɔ́ dɩɛ, sɛ ɛ 'fa esika ɛ man ehianfʋɛ'n-mɔ a, n'ɛ man wɔ́ manngʋn'n muonun mɔ ɛ nʋn yɩ́ nanndɩ'n nwun nun.
translated: If you want to get wealth, you must get it through hard work, and if you get it through hard work, you will enjoy it and it will be sweet.

ref: He trusted God, so let God save him, if he wants to. He even said he was God's Son.”
text: Ɔ'a susu yɩ́ ti ɔ'a gua Nyanmɩan asʋ. Yɩ́ nwan: ‘Nyanmɩan Awa y'ɔ le mɩ́n’. Sɛ Nyanmɩan kulo yɩ́ a, anɩn ɔ lé yɩ́ ti kɩkaala!”
translated: He thought to himself that he would go to Nyanmɩan's house. He said: ‘Nyanmɩan's wife is beautiful’. When he got to Nyanmɩan's house, they said he was a liar!

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Mɔ Zozi nwunlin yɩ́ sɔ'n, yɩ́ nwan: “Bɛ bu Nyanmɩan kpɔmanfʋɛ biala sʋanlan lɩka kʋalaa. Nan yɩ́ muonun yɩ́ kulo nɩn asʋ menian'n-mɔ nʋn yɩ́ nvilie'n-mɔ yɛ̂ bɛ mmu man yɩ́ sʋanlan ɔ.”
translated: The chief Zozi was sitting on his stool, saying: “Our Nyanmɩan, the great king, has sent a strong war messenger to us. He said that if we do not immediately give him food, he will come and burn our village.”

ref: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi kɔ sa kɔ hɔ Galile asubula nɩn anʋan ɛlɔ biekun. Menian dɔʋn kɔ kpʋ yɩ́, ɔ kɔ hehele bɛ́ ninnge.
translated: He went to his hometown, and on the Sabbath he began to teach in the synagogue. Many who heard him were astonished, saying, “Where did this man get these things? What is this wisdom that has been given to him?

ref: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
text: “Ɛ 'nwun like mɔ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n soman yɩ́'n: Ɛlɛhʋn kʋn mɩnlɩan kʋn kɔ jasʋ ngɛlɛmɔ tutun kpa, ɔ 'kɔ a kpʋnndɛ menian maan bɛ di junman yɩ́ divɩn ebo nɩn asʋ.
translated: The chiefs like Anwunno Belemgbin told their people: They gave them money and cloth to go and buy guns to fight, but they brought back only empty words and lies.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Mɔ belemgbin Elɔdɩ nwunlin kɛ ngɛlɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ a laka yɩ́'n, ɔ fɛlɩ ɛya kpa. Ɔ tɩtɩlɩ cɩan'n mɔ ngɛlɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ nwunlin nzalama'n dede a, anɩn ɔ'a nwun kɛ batʋnman'n ka kaan di afʋɛ nnyuan. Yɩ́ ti, ɔ kɔ sʋan yɩ́ nzalafʋ'n-mɔ Betelehɛmʋn nʋn kulo'n-mɔ kʋalaa mɔ bɛ mantan mantan yɩ́'n-mɔ asʋ kɛ bɛ hɔ́ hún mmatʋnman mmelenzua mɔ b'a li afʋɛ nnyuan'n, ɔ nʋn bɛ́ mɔ bɛ lili man afʋɛ nnyuan'n kʋalaatin.
translated: When Herod the king heard these things, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him. And when he had gathered all the chief priests and scribes of the people together, he demanded of them where Christ should be born. And they said unto him, In Bethlehem of Judaea: for thus it is written by the prophet, And thou Bethlehem, in the land of Juda, art not the least among the princes of Juda: for out of thee shall come a Governor, that shall rule my people Israel.

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.

translated: When they had finished eating, he took some more and gave it to his disciples to eat, and they all ate and were satisfied.

translated: Hello, I have come to ask you a question, and I say: “You are Falisifʋɛ's child, I hear. Can you tell me where your father's compound is?”

text: Sɛ Nyanmɩan muonun a mgbɛ man tɛmʋn sɔ nɩn asʋ a, sʋanlan fɩ́ɩ́ nnyan man yɩ́ ti. Nan yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ mɔ ɔ'a kpa bɛ́ ɔ'a yɔ yɩ́ dɩɛ'n-mɔ dunman nun, ɔ'a kpɛ cɩan sɔ'n-mɔ asʋ.
translated: If you want to eat food then you must first prepare it, then you can eat. You must first prepare food before you eat it, then you can chew and swallow it.

ref: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
text: Mɔ *Falisifʋɛ'n-mɔ tɩlɩ kɛ Zozi a hʋala *Sadusifʋɛ'n-mɔ'n, bɛ kɔ yia ɛbɛlɛ.
translated: The chief Falisifɔ's family and Sadusifɔ's family are related, they are from the same clan.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Mɔ belemgbin Elɔdɩ nwunlin kɛ ngɛlɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ a laka yɩ́'n, ɔ fɛlɩ ɛya kpa. Ɔ tɩtɩlɩ cɩan'n mɔ ngɛlɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ nwunlin nzalama'n dede a, anɩn ɔ'a nwun kɛ batʋnman'n ka kaan di afʋɛ nnyuan. Yɩ́ ti, ɔ kɔ sʋan yɩ́ nzalafʋ'n-mɔ Betelehɛmʋn nʋn kulo'n-mɔ kʋalaa mɔ bɛ mantan mantan yɩ́'n-mɔ asʋ kɛ bɛ hɔ́ hún mmatʋnman mmelenzua mɔ b'a li afʋɛ nnyuan'n, ɔ nʋn bɛ́ mɔ bɛ lili man afʋɛ nnyuan'n kʋalaatin.
translated: When Herod the king heard these things, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him. And when he had gathered all the chief priests and scribes of the people together, he demanded of them where Christ should be born. And they said unto him, In Bethlehem of Judaea: for thus it is written by the prophet, And thou Bethlehem, in the land of Juda, art not the least among the princes of Juda: for out of thee shall come a Governor, that shall rule my people Israel.

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Ɛ bɔ wɔ́ nwʋn apoo, di mʋa yɩ tabua ebue'n m'ɔ la wɔ́ nyɩn nɩn asʋ'n, nán a nwun asɩ a yɩ tabua munmuin kaan'n m'ɔ la wɔ́ manngʋn nɩn anyɩn nɩn asʋ'n.
translated: When they had finished eating, he took some of the leftover food and gave it to his disciples saying that they should give some to the crowd because they were hungry.

ref: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi kɔ bu anyɩnndala kʋn biekun kɔ hele bɛ́. Yɩ́ nwan: “Ɛ 'nwun like mɔ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n soman yɩ́'n: Ɔ soman sʋanlan kʋn mɔ ɔ'a gua *bele yɩ́ ebo nɩn asʋ ɔ.
translated: When they had finished, Zozi and the people of the land gathered together. He said to them: “My late father, Anwunno Belemgbin, when he was still alive, said that when he dies, he should be buried in a big pit.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ sʋanlan m'ɔ le bie'n, bɛ 'man yɩ́ dɔʋn biekun. Nan sʋanlan kʋsʋ m'ɔ le man fɩ́ɩ́'n, bɛ 'tʋa yɩ́ bɛ de kaan'n m'ɔ le'n.
translated: When a hunter kills an animal and it is not very big, they do not divide it. But when the animal killed is very big, they divide it into equal parts.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: Ɛmɔ hɔ́ ndɛ-ndɛ bɛ hán héle yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ ɔ fi ewue nun ɔ'a tinnge. Sian dɩɛ, ɔ lɛ kɔ a nwʋnndɛ ɛmɔ Galile mân nɩn anun. Ɛlɔ yɛ̂ ɛmɔ 'nwun yɩ́ ɔ. Ngalɩɛ'n mɔ mɩ́n nwan mɩn 'kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ nɩn anɩn.”
translated: This is why all the things that he has done since he began his work are written down. Now, he went to Galilee and preached the good news.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.

translated: When they finished eating, they went to the farm to work, and on their way back from the farm, they did not meet any bad person on the road.

ref: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi kɔ bu anyɩnndala kʋn biekun kɔ hele bɛ́. Yɩ́ nwan: “Ɛ 'nwun like mɔ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n soman yɩ́'n: Ɔ soman sʋanlan kʋn mɔ ɔ'a gua *bele yɩ́ ebo nɩn asʋ ɔ.
translated: When the day broke, Zozi and his hunters went to the bush and came back empty-handed. He said: "I have a dream this morning, that Anwunno Belemgbin Man was giving me something: He gave me a live animal and it was not even afraid of me at all."

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ sʋanlan m'ɔ le bie'n, bɛ 'man yɩ́ dɔʋn biekun. Nan sʋanlan kʋsʋ m'ɔ le man fɩ́ɩ́'n, bɛ 'tʋa yɩ́ bɛ de kaan'n m'ɔ le'n.
translated: When a hunter kills an animal, they do not give it to someone else to divide. But when the hunter's dog kills an animal, they make the person who is with it divide it.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”

translated: It was while he was still speaking that a crowd came, for he was at the place called Gethsemane. Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them.
